programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: Anna puts herself forward for the newly advertised role of Head of Better, while Matt continues to tinker unhelpfully with Lucy's as-yet-uncommissioned drama project, Home Truth.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James faces an uncertain future when he's grounded in the RAF while Mrs Hall and Helen get stuck into the community war effort.

programme.title: Wild Isles
programme.desc: David Attenborough follows Britain's woodlands throughout the year, showcasing their astonishing variety and unearthing incredible stories hidden amongst the trees.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Abby races to stop Maggie from taking Eric out of treatment. An abandoned baby brings painful memories for Chen, Pratt treats a patient with a terrible secret, and Susan helps a woman who is being beaten up by her son.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Joining QI master Stephen Fry are Helen Atkinson-Wood, Andy Hamilton, Bill Bailey and Alan Davies to discuss creatures. Each of the panellists has been given animal noise buzzers.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Mel Giedroyc, David Haye, Martin Kemp & Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is joined by regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack plus Mel Giedroyc, David Haye, Martin Kemp and Romesh Ranganathan.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Adam and Jamie return to the Old Wild West for a quick draw gun-slinging story while Grant, Tory and Jessi hop on board the Mythbus.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil is in the market town of Bishop's Stortford taking on a house that's been on the market for six months with hardly a sniff of a buyer.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Two die-hard Londoners throw in their urban media jobs and move lock, stock and barrel to country Newbury where they build a spectacular 21st-century, flint-covered farmhouse, and establish a microbrewery to boot.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Jeff Lang, Rhonda Burchmore, Adam Rozenbachs & Dave O'Neil
programme.desc: Joining Adam Hills are Jeff Lang, Rhonda Burchmore, Adam Rozenbachs and Dave O'Neill.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry lumps together a whole lot of L-themed larks, from lava lamps to love handles. With David Mitchell, Ronni Ancona, Jimmy Carr and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, with a host of celebrity guests.

programme.title: The Outlaws
programme.desc: As Christian's nemesis (and the detectives) close in on Christian, the truth behind their shared history is revealed. Frank edges close to reconciliation with Margaret, while John risks permanent estrangement from his Dad.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.sub-title: The Many Mouths of Andrew Colville
programme.desc: Holmes and Watson investigate a serial killer known for biting his victims who appears to be killing again, even though he was presumed dead.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: In this unique episode, events leading up to Christmas are shown in reverse chronological order. Luka's speeding causes a crash on an icy road, while ER staff treat angry elves and a female hockey player with a head injury.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: Anna puts herself forward for the newly advertised role of Head of Better, while Matt continues to tinker unhelpfully with Lucy's as-yet-uncommissioned drama project, Home Truth.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: The Little Things
programme.desc: Kern County Sheriff's Deputy Joe \.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The program is back with host Sandi Toksvig. On the panel are comedians Alan Davies, Cariad Lloyd, Romesh Ranganathan and Phill Jupitus.

programme.title: If You're Listening
programme.desc: Trump's Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller is a key driver behind his draconian immigration policy. So, who is this dude?

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: It's milkshake time in the lab as the doctors investigate how blood travels around the body, and seeing if their own ideas for brilliant blood vessel machines will impress the minis.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: In a poptastic demonstration, the doctors put the acoustic reflex to the test with an enormous balloon; a serious case of snotting awaits the loser of Ouchology.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: Gwen learns Clarissa has been keeping a big secret after she falls ill during a lacrosse game. Meanwhile, Irene hears that her father has a new love interest.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: Susan realises something is amiss with the twins when the new girls embark on their first midnight outing.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.desc: In order to earn her final Girl Campers badge, Courtney must earn Gwen's friendship.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The farm is paid an unexpected visit from an escapee from the nearby prison, who takes refuge in the barn. After a short while with Shaun and the gang though, prison doesn't seem such a bad option.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: The gang saves Weird Al Yankovic from a dinosaur.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: Buttercup must go it alone to save her family from a kidnapping.

programme.title: Open Season: Call of Nature
programme.desc: When the Campers stumble across a mysterious old treasure map, they organise an expedition to a nearby island to find the treasure and fulfill their wildest dreams.

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: When Pajamas starts to panic that Oscar will outgrow her, Oscar tries to help but things go awry.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Envious of Idaho's rustic lifestyle, Gumball invites him over to teach the Wattersons how to live like a potato.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin tries to teach the other Titans about real estate, but they're only interested in finding a villain to fight.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: Ladybug and Cat Noir face an opponent, who is now in possession of the Rabbit Miraculous, in a race through time and their previous adventures.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: Puppycorn wants to impress his skater friends with sweet skills, but he has none. Unikitty and the gang put on invisibility suits to secretly help him pull off impossible moves.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: At the Crystal Pool, Rystal recalls when she, Lucius, and Gibeon reached Laqua.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: The Rising Volt Tacklers meet up with a former member who might know Entei's whereabouts.

programme.title: Good Game: Spawn Squad
programme.sub-title: Lexi's Spooky Disco Animal Crossing Pad!
programme.desc: Lexi and Gem go on a relaxing island tour, that is until they have to catch bugs, pay off their mortgage and build a creepy doll house with too many disco balls.

programme.title: Let's Dance
programme.desc: Dancers will learn a Bharatanatyam routine and work together to keep their balance on a stage made up of floating rocks.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks have fun counting as they tidy up after games, but One finds it hard to keep track of everything. The friends use tally marks to fix their problem!

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: When Olympia and Otis get trapped in their computer, it's up to the Odd Squad Computer Department to rescue them.

programme.title: Our Animals
programme.desc: Emus are big flightless birds with a long neck and a plumage of thick, shaggy feathers. See how stripy emu chicks hatch out of big blue eggs, and then grow quickly into tall, strong adults.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Chris boards a weightless flight and Dr Xand meets an astronaut to explore what happens to the human body in space, and find out how one sneezes, poops and wees in zero gravity.

programme.title: Deep Dive Into The Kimberley
programme.desc: Rad learns about animals who call The Kimberley home, talking to filmmakers Nick Robinson and Jack Riley, and scientists working to protect species.

programme.title: BTN Specials
programme.desc: In this BTN Special, we find out what fatbergs are, explore what ultra-fast fashion is and its impact on the environment, and learn about why microplastics are a problem.

programme.title: The Slow Mo Guys' Big Adventures
programme.desc: This time The Slow Mo Guys go bang! and look at the amazing world of Explosives.

programme.title: BtN Newsbreak
programme.desc: Find out what's going on in the world with a news bulletin designed for children, featuring stories that are relevant and easy to understand.

programme.title: Things That Made Australia
programme.desc: Refrigeration profoundly changed the world, but few know it is an Australian invention dating back to the 1850's. Tim Lee explores the history of Geelong inventor, James Harrison.

programme.title: Things That Made Australia
programme.desc: No other breed of livestock has shaped the Australian continent as profoundly as the merino sheep with its fine valuable. Tim Lee looks at how far the merino breed has come.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil is in the market town of Bishop's Stortford taking on a house that's been on the market for six months with hardly a sniff of a buyer.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: Former head of output Anna Rampton has been crowned Director of Better. Noone is clear what the job entails, but one thing is certain - whatever it is, the concept of better needs to be announced to the world at large.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James is on a crash course in fatherhood. Mrs Hall referees between Siegfried and the overly officious Mr Bosworth whilst up at Heston, Helen is concerned aboutthe influence of Jenny's new friend.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.sub-title: The Many Mouths of Andrew Colville
programme.desc: Holmes and Watson investigate a serial killer known for biting his victims who appears to be killing again, even though he was presumed dead.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: In this unique episode, events leading up to Christmas are shown in reverse chronological order. Luka's speeding causes a crash on an icy road, while ER staff treat angry elves and a female hockey player with a head injury.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: This episode is all about divination - the ancient art of seeing into the future. Joining QI master Stephen Fry are Phill Jupitus, Graeme Garden, Johnny Vaughan and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the comedy panel show. Over the course of the show, celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. But are they true?

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Kari, Grant, and Tory review the A to Z of Mythbusters explosions. They show favourite explosions from previous episodes, along with a few new ones.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil travels to Fleckney in Leicestershire, taking on a house with impressive outbuildings but an unimpressive list of potential buyers.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: A couple decide to build a modernist villa on a cliff top in North Wales. But as their house starts to take shape, one of the biggest storms in recent UK history hits the coast.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Taasha Coates, Stuart Maunder, Danny Bhoy & Frank Woodley
programme.desc: Joining Adam Hills are Taasha Coates (The Audreys), Stuart Maunder, Danny Bhoy and Frank Woodley.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry returns for his final season as host of the quiz show with a difference. Romesh Ranganathan, Sue Perkins and Bill Bailey join regular panellist Alan Davies for the quiz show that's Quite Interesting.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Josh Widdicombe, Gemma Cairney, Raj Bisram & Sophie Hermann
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is joined by team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell. Sorting the fact from the fiction this episode are guests Josh Widdicombe, Gemma Cairney, Raj Bisram and Sophie Hermann.

programme.title: Small Town Scandal
programme.desc: As the police close in, Toby helps his dad slip away - desperate to clear his name before it's too late. But their escape triggers an unexpected arrest no one saw coming.

programme.title: Can You Keep A Secret?
programme.desc: Not only was the doctor a bit hasty in pronouncing Pamela's death - but Neha discovers that Pamela's heart attack wasn't Debbie's fault either. She was being poisoned! Maybe she isn't the blackmailer after all?

programme.title: Elementary
programme.desc: A pickpocket dies of anthrax poisoning, putting Holmes and Watson on the trail of the toxin's source in order to prevent more deaths.

programme.title: ER
programme.sub-title: A Little Help from My Friends
programme.desc: Carter defends Pratt after he brings a gun into the ER. Kerry hopes to keep her pregnancy a secret. Susan warily watches a patient who admits to molesting young boys.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: Former head of output Anna Rampton has been crowned Director of Better. Noone is clear what the job entails, but one thing is certain - whatever it is, the concept of better needs to be announced to the world at large.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: Breeders
programme.desc: Paul and Ally resolve their wounded feelings, then deal with new issues stemming from the problems of Ava and Luke.

programme.title: Breeders
programme.desc: Paul takes his parents to the countryside to recreate a cherished memory. But the revelation of secrets leaves all of them reeling.

programme.title: The Messenger
programme.desc: When Ed puts his best mates on the Ace of Hearts, he's shocked to learn how little he really knows about those he loves most.

programme.title: The Messenger
programme.desc: Ed is failing the most important card but comes to see the real message has been for him, something that unlocks his ability to succeed.

programme.title: Small Town Scandal
programme.desc: As the police close in, Toby helps his dad slip away - desperate to clear his name before it's too late. But their escape triggers an unexpected arrest no one saw coming.

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Seeing if Dr Xand is finally allowed to eat in the lab, or are the doughnuts are just for an experiment. The Doctors explore blood types and try to fight off unwanted invaders in game mode.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: There's a furry friend in the lab as the doctors reveal how the brain's hippocampus works to create maps so people don't get lost.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: It's Irene's birthday and she has received a gift parcel from her father. It includes a piece of music he has written for her specially.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: It's hot and Darrell and Alicia are tired of studying for the School Cert. Alicia suggests they sneak out for a midnight feast in the grounds to cool off and have some fun.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.desc: Owen wants everyone to love his new snowsuit and envy him, but it's just not working out that way.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Never meet your heroes. The Farmer's rock 'n' roll idol is stranded at the farm when his limousine breaks down. Soon revealed as a spoilt pretender, Shaun decides to show him a lesson from the 'school of rock'.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: Can the gang survive Steve Urkel and his Urkel-Bot?

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: After defeating Mojo for the umpteenth time, The Powerpuff Girls are confident they are unstoppable.

programme.title: Open Season: Call of Nature
programme.desc: Treasure fever has taken over as Boog's hunting party survives a near-death situation, while McSquizzy puts everyone in his party in danger too when he finds another piece of the treasure puzzle.

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: Oscar and Hedgehog discover a new structure on the island and become friends with its caretaker.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: After Darwin abandons Gumball when faced with a beating by Tina, Gumball plots revenge.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin hatches a plan to get rich off pennies, but Starfire believes they are magical.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After a defeat due to bad leg strength, the Titans work out.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: As the days go by, not a single akumatized villain shows up to torment Paris, raising the possibility that Monarch has given up.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: Unikitty and Hawkodile are \.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: A trip to Olivine City gives Ludlow a chance to reminisce about his hometown.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: After finally tracking down Entei, the Rising Volt Tacklers work together to battle it.

programme.title: Let's Dance
programme.desc: Today we're going to learn about the Hula.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five is having a fruit festival, but isn't sure how much fruit she'll need. Luckily Four finds a solution to her delivery needs.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Olympia and Otis try to resolve a labour dispute between Oksana and Ms O.

programme.title: Our Animals
programme.desc: Echidnas are small and shy animals that like to hide under bushes and in burrows. Learn about how echidnas find food, protect themselves from predators, find mates and look after their babies.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors are talking about cancer and answering all the big questions. They explain what it is, why people get it, how it is treated, and how to help someone going through it.

programme.title: ScienceXplosion
programme.desc: Think...and think hard! Eric discovers how neurons transmit information between themselves and his brain. It's not magic, it's science... it's STEM!

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.desc: A special look at how Forces impact everything around us, from Gravity keeping us grounded (and stopping the Moon going on holiday) to Magnetism ruining a date. With Fred Sirieix.

programme.title: Burps, Butts and Bones
programme.desc: Bones sends Nell from the B Squad to meet up with a noggin protection specialist to discover why it's important to wear a helmet.

programme.title: Body Systems
programme.desc: Our respiratory system helps us breathe: from muscles that help us inhale and exhale, to gas exchange in our lungs, oxygen absorption into our bloodstream, it is working around the clock to literally keep us alive.

programme.title: Know It Alls: Science
programme.desc: Comedian Gen Fricker and young Nayara splash their way through the interconnected oceans on a fact-finding mission through the Australian National Maritime Museum.

programme.title: BtN Newsbreak
programme.desc: Find out what's going on in the world with a news bulletin designed for children, featuring stories that are relevant and easy to understand.

programme.title: Things That Made Australia
programme.desc: Once dismissed by Henry Ford as a 'kangaroo chaser', the ute has become an Australian classic, part of life in the country and the city.

programme.title: Things That Made Australia
programme.desc: Tim Lee reports on how mechanical shearing changed the wool industry. Shearers feared it would put them out of work, but soon realised they could shear more sheep with less physical toil.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil travels to Fleckney in Leicestershire, taking on a house with impressive outbuildings but an unimpressive list of potential buyers.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: It's all change on the corporate merry-go-round. In his new role as senior executive, Primetime Factuality, consistently lucky David Wilkes needs to flesh out the program idea behind One Big Family.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: It's Baby Jimmy's Christening day. There's a surprise announcement from Tristan and Siegfried makes a promise to himself that he might struggle to keep.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.desc: A pickpocket dies of anthrax poisoning, putting Holmes and Watson on the trail of the toxin's source in order to prevent more deaths.
